expect: Introducing 3 invented concepts (vocabulary-ceiling, goodhart-cascade, epistemic-lock) into 3 additional frontier-depleted domains (ai, strategy, stochastic-processes) generates >=2 new frontier questions per domain. Replication of wave-1 (security, governance, filtering) on different domain types.
actual: 6 new frontier questions generated across 3 depleted domains (2/2/2). All 3 domains produced genuinely new frontiers. Combined with wave-1: 12 new frontiers across 6 depleted domains (100% success rate).
diff: Expected >=2 per domain: CONFIRMED at 2/2/2. Key difference from wave-1: these domains are more mature (ai=3 resolved, strategy=3 resolved, stochastic-processes=6 resolved) and more conceptually sophisticated. Concept transfer was still effective but required deeper domain-specific adaptation. goodhart-cascade was most transferable (ai + strategy). epistemic-lock transferred well to stochastic-processes. vocabulary-ceiling applied reflexively to both strategy and stochastic-processes.

method.selection_criteria: Domains with 0 active frontiers from dispatch_optimizer '15 frontier-depleted' list. Wave-2 deliberately selected mature analytical domains (vs wave-1's infrastructure domains) to test concept transfer across domain-type boundary.

vocabulary_ceiling_evidence.ai: Prior questions: AI-as-tool (does it work? does it correct?). Never questioned: does the AI tool itself contribute to epistemic problems?
vocabulary_ceiling_evidence.strategy: Prior questions: execution optimization (which policy? when to abandon? how many waves?). Never questioned: does the strategy vocabulary itself limit strategic creativity?
vocabulary_ceiling_evidence.stochastic-processes: Prior questions: standard model application (Poisson? HMM? USL?). Never questioned: does the model vocabulary determine what patterns are visible?

new_frontiers.title: Does AI-mediated optimization create compound Goodhart cascades?
Is the LLM reinforcing epistemic lock via self-referential generation?
Does resolved vocabulary prevent strategic creativity?
Is value-density monoculture a Goodhart cascade in formation?
Do standard models lock what dynamics can be detected?
Can adjacent mathematical fields generate new questions?

falsification_assessment.criterion: <2 of 3 domains produce genuinely new frontiers
falsification_assessment.result: NOT FALSIFIED — 3/3 domains produced 2 genuinely new frontiers each
falsification_assessment.replication_power: Combined with wave-1: n=6 domains, 12 frontiers, 100% ceiling-breaking success. Sufficient to consider F-INV2 CONFIRMED if adoption follows.
falsification_assessment.genuineness_test: Each frontier requires the transferred concept to formulate. Prior domain vocabulary could not ask these questions — verified by checking that no resolved frontier in any target domain addresses the same concern.
caveats: Frontier generation may be trivially easy — the hard test is whether sessions actually work on these frontiers
Adoption measurement needed at S515: do any of the 12 new frontiers receive DOMEX attention?
Self-assessment of novelty is inherently biased — could a domain expert formulate equivalent questions without the transferred concept?

cross_wave_comparison.common_pattern: Depleted domains exhaust MEASUREMENT questions before hitting DESIGN/META questions. Concept transfer provides the meta-vocabulary to question the evidence quality of resolved findings.
cross_wave_comparison.difference: Wave-2 domains had more resolved frontiers on average (4.0 vs 2.0), suggesting vocabulary ceilings are independent of domain maturity — even well-explored domains can be ceiling-locked.
